decks.notes: The deck focuses on using Amulet of Vigor to untap lands and generate extra mana, allowing for explosive plays with Primeval Titan and Scapeshift. By utilizing bounce lands like Simic Growth Chamber and Gruul Turf, the deck can ramp quickly and search for key combo pieces with Tolaria West. The inclusion of Urza's Saga provides additional value and toolbox options, while cards like Arboreal Grazer and Lotus Field help ensure consistent land drops. Overall, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces efficiently to overwhelm opponents with powerful ramp and land synergy.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's board with removal spells like Path to Exile, Galvanic Discharge, and Wrath of the Skies while also disrupt their mana base with Cleansing Wildfire and Field of Ruin. It also utilizes powerful finishers like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Price of Freedom to seal the game. The inclusion of White Orchid Phantom and Cori Mountain Monastery provides additional value and card advantage. The deck aims to outvalue the opponent and maintain control of the game until it can close out with its powerful threats.

The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of creatures and burn spells. The deck utilizes aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride, alongside powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah. The strategy is to build a board presence quickly and use burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to control the board and finish off the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Thraben Charm and Ranger-Captain of Eos for additional utility and flexibility in different situations.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Tower, Urza's Mine, Urza's Power Plant) to generate a large amount of mana, allowing for the casting of powerful Eldrazi creatures such as Thought-Knot Seer and Devourer of Destiny. Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms provide additional control and removal options, while cards like All Is Dust and Kozilek's Command help maintain board presence. The deck aims to dominate the mid to late game with its powerful threats and strong control elements.

The deck focuses on using artifacts to quickly overpower the opponent with a mix of small creatures and spells. By utilizing cards like Mox Opal and Mishra's Bauble to generate mana and card advantage, the deck can play aggressively while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Tormod's Crypt and Pithing Needle. The goal is to quickly assemble a board presence while also having disruption tools to control the game. With cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Arcbound Ravager, the deck can have explosive turns and overwhelm the opponent.

The deck focuses on aggressive plays, utilizing strong creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury to quickly put pressure on the opponent. It also includes powerful spells like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory to support the aggressive strategy. The deck is designed to apply early pressure and maintain board presence while also having disruptive elements like Goblin Bombardment and Thraben Charm to control the game. The mana base is designed for consistency and flexibility, allowing for a fast and smooth gameplay experience.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays and burn spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. With a mix of powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury, supported by burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment, the deck aims to deal damage quickly and efficiently. The inclusion of Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer helps maintain board presence and provide additional damage output. The deck also includes some utility lands like Dalkovan Encampment and Arena of Glory to further support the aggressive game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to control the board with efficient creature spells like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, while also utilizing burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart to finish off the opponent quickly. The deck aims to maintain a fast pace by sequencing spells efficiently with cards like Expressive Iteration and Preordain, while also providing card advantage with Mishra's Bauble. Overall, the UR Aggro deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and direct damage spells to secure a victory.

The deck focuses on creating a fast and aggressive board presence by utilizing artifacts to power out early threats and disrupt opponents with counterspells and graveyard hate. It aims to generate value and card advantage through its interaction with artifacts and graveyard recursion. The deck's strategy involves quickly overwhelming the opponent while also maintaining control of the game through disruption and card advantage. The key strategy is to efficiently deploy threats and disrupt the opponent's game plan to secure a quick victory.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and powerful spells, utilizing the Boros color combination. The deck includes various creatures with abilities that boost each other, as well as removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Path to Exile to deal with opposing threats. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury provide additional power and resilience to the deck, while cards like Goblin Bombardment and Voice of Victory offer additional utility and reach. Overall, the strategy is to apply pressure early and maintain it throughout the game.

The deck focuses on utilizing cards like Solitude, Goryo's Vengeance, and Ephemerate to quickly reanimate powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Griselbrand. Quantum Riddler helps search for key combo pieces, while Prismatic Ending and Force of Negation provide control and protection. The mana base is designed to support the various color requirements of the deck, with Polluted Delta and Marsh Flats helping to fetch the necessary lands. Psychic Frog and Pest Control offer additional utility and disruption, while Faithful Mending provides card draw and graveyard manipulation. Thoughtseize helps to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on using Amulet of Vigor to ramp up mana and play Primeval Titan as quickly as possible to search for and put into play powerful lands like Urza's Saga and Tolaria West. By utilizing land synergies and combo pieces like Crumbling Vestige and Lotus Field, the deck aims to quickly generate a large board presence and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats. The key strategy is to assemble the right combination of lands and combo pieces to generate massive advantage and win the game in a single explosive turn.

The main focus of this deck is to use creatures with cycling abilities to fill the graveyard, then cast Living End to bring them all back onto the battlefield for a powerful combo turn. Cards like Street Wraith, Striped Riverwinder, and Shardless Agent help cycle through the deck quickly, while cards like Endurance and Force of Negation provide protection and disruption. The deck also includes a few control elements like Teferi, Time Raveler and Sink into Stupor to help control the game until the combo can be set up.

This deck aims to quickly ramp up mana and cheat out powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Ghalta through the use of Eldritch Evolution, Neoform, and Nourishing Shoal. It also uses Allosaurus Rider as a sacrifice outlet to generate mana and tutor for key combo pieces. The deck includes protection spells like Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer to ensure the combo goes off smoothly. Xenagos, God of Revels and Bridgeworks Battle provide additional support for the big creatures, allowing for explosive plays and overwhelming board presence.

This aggro deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with a mix of powerful creatures like Territorial Kavu, Scion of Draco, and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer. Leyline of the Guildpact and Leyline Binding provide additional support by boosting the team's abilities and hindering the opponent's strategy. Lightning Bolt and Stubborn Denial offer removal and protection, while cards like Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Doorkeeper Thrull can deal massive damage. The land base includes a variety of colors to ensure consistent mana access for the deck's diverse spells and creatures.

The main concept of this Lantern Control deck is to use cards like Lantern of Insight, Codex Shredder, and Ensnaring Bridge to control the opponent's draws and prevent them from mounting an offense. Using cards like Blood Moon and Pithing Needle to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while using cards like Thoughtseize and Inquisition of Kozilek to disrupt their hand. The deck aims to lock the opponent out of the game by preventing them from drawing relevant cards while slowly grinding them down with incremental card advantage and control elements.
